title: The Sick Village
timelinePosition: Two days after Chapter 3. The party has ridden south through ordinary-looking country marked by scattered sickness and stronger readings, not open collapse.
openLocation: A still-functioning village south of Millhaven where sickness is spreading but has not yet been named for what it is.
transport: Southbound on Thalassian horses; on foot inside the village.
immediateObjective: Assess an ordinary-looking outbreak site before it quietly becomes another Millhaven.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Jaina perception only.
No explicit organization naming before the chapter itself earns only local conspiracy evidence.
No thaw, trust, or softness between Jaina and Sylvanas; superiority weakens, irritation stays.

povKnowsAtOpen: The survivor girl's testimony plus the follow-up tracking pass confirmed Millhaven's dead marched north toward Quel'Thalas.
Millhaven appears to be the first village on this road fully claimed; the country beyond still looks ordinary enough to hide milder outbreaks.
Her sensitivity lets the party notice trouble earlier than anyone else would, but not early enough to save a village once the grain is already moving.
The contamination is physical, pervasive, and lethal.
The magical signature is necromantic in the broad Kirin Tor sense, even if the banned applied theory behind it sits outside her education.
One survivor girl was found in Millhaven and sent north under Neral's escort on horseback.
Neral has already departed north and is no longer with the riding party.
The old seller and the girl's testimony together produced one weak clue about a newer grain seller, but nothing conclusive.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: How it first gets into people.
Whether the old grain seller matters.
Whether this village can still be saved.
Whether someone is steering the outbreak or it is spreading on its own.
The exact point where sickness becomes the dead getting back up.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No grain confirmation before Jaina earns it from patient patterning.
No cult proof before the granary scene.
No retroactive certainty about the old seller.
No suggestion that Jaina is ignorant of necromancy in the broad sense; her gap is in banned practice, not in knowing the subject exists.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Grain is confirmed as what carries the plague into people, and the road and storehouses are how it spreads.
A live village watcher existed inside the sickness pattern and died by poison before he could be questioned.
Granary markings and ledgers tie the grain route southwest through Andorhal, making it the next objective.
The old seller / newer grain-man anomalies remain weak scraps in the file rather than proof.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: If Jaina can name how the sickness is getting into people quickly enough, she can still keep this village from becoming another Millhaven.
pov.surfaceObjective: Find the entry path before the living village tips over into the same silence and bodies.
pov.pressureSource: The contamination is buried inside ordinary routines, and Sylvanas keeps seeing the human tells Jaina keeps treating as background.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina keeps reaching for the magical answer first and treating Sylvanas's people-read as supporting evidence instead of part of the core method.
pov.chapterTest: Every lead that looks purely arcane opens back into food, labor, timing, and village habit.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep driving toward a neat magical answer, or let Sylvanas's read of fear, routine, and supply reshape where Jaina looks.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina leaves with more evidence and less control. She still wants the clean mechanism, but she stops pretending Sylvanas is just escort muscle.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 5 opens with Jaina quicker to bring Sylvanas the messy human details instead of filing them as background noise.

throughPov.visibleFunction: The woman who can read fear, routine, and supply faster than Jaina can.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: A sharp soldier, but still someone working below the level of the real answer.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Sylvanas keeps landing on the pressure points Jaina misses, which forces Jaina to admit this is not lesser intelligence, just a different cut through the same problem.
throughPov.interactionRule: Jaina can use Sylvanas's name in her head easily now, but the tone stays tense. By chapter close she should sound less like she is lecturing downward.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Two specialists sharing a road because neither can finish the job alone.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The village proves their work only holds when Jaina reads the arcane layer and Sylvanas reads the human one at the same time.
team.doNotOverstateRule: This is not trust or softness. It is the first time Jaina admits the other half of the method.

entries.rule: Still present with the riding party.
Still present with the riding party.
Not in this chapter. She has already departed north with the warning relay and the survivor girl.
Not in this chapter. She has already departed north under Neral's escort.
Local one-chapter authority figure for this outbreak site.
Local one-chapter witness to the sickness pattern; does not travel forward.
Local one-chapter logistics witness; useful only for this village's supply history.
Local one-chapter operative tied to the grain stores; dies before he can be turned into a continuing witness.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 5 opens with the same smaller riding party following the grain road southwest.

scenes.title: The Village That Is Not Dead Yet
scenes.sceneFunction: Show the plague while the village still thinks it is ordinary sickness, and split the work between Jaina's sickroom read and Sylvanas's people read.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The contamination is real; how it gets into people is still unknown.
scenes.arcPressure: Jaina keeps reaching for a purely magical answer while Sylvanas is already reading the human bottlenecks that make the outbreak legible.

enrichment.detail: Lordaeron village texture should stay practical rather than gothic: timber-and-stone buildings, bakehouse smoke, wet road mud, rough market noise, and ordinary grain traffic. That normal bread-and-market baseline makes the buried sickness feel embedded in daily life instead of already post-apocalyptic.

beats.conflict: This settlement is still functioning, and that makes the danger harder to read because the locals see sickness, not plague.

outcome.summary: The party reaches a living site, but Jaina immediately understands the wrongness is already inside ordinary life.

beats.valueShift: investigative focus -> ordinary life with buried wrongness
newOnPageInformation: The same contamination can be observed before open collapse.
Most villagers still think they are dealing with an ugly run of sickness, not a spreading weapon.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How the sickness is getting in.
Whether intervention can do more than delay death.
beats.transitionIntent: dialogue hook: the militia captain's resistance has to be solved before Jaina can touch the sickroom.

beats.goal: Secure access without provoking a jurisdiction fight.
beats.conflict: The militia captain has no reason to trust armed elves in what still looks, to him, like a bad local sickness run.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets access by confirming Millhaven's fate and forcing him to take the possibility seriously, but local trust is thin and disbelief still slows everyone.

beats.valueShift: ordinary life with buried wrongness -> access under pressure
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas can read exhausted authority and bargain with it instead of bulldozing it.
Jaina sees diplomacy where she expected force.
Jaina realizes Sylvanas is solving the room instead of dominating it, which is not what she expected from a soldier.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether what they learn here will generalize beyond this village.
sensoryAnchors: Millhaven's fate landing in the room
beats.transitionIntent: action continuation: Jaina peels toward the infirmary while Sylvanas works doors, stores, and frightened locals for the village's change history.

beats.goal: Map the contamination in living bodies.
beats.conflict: Jaina is looking at the same necromantic pattern she saw as residue in Millhaven, but now alive and spreading through tissue in real time.

outcome.summary: She can describe the stages, but not yet how it first gets in.

beats.valueShift: access under pressure -> something is getting into them somehow
newOnPageInformation: Early cases cluster contamination in the gut.
Only a handful of cases are sick enough to show the pattern spreading out from the gut, which means the body is taking it in and carrying it further.
The village medic has been treating it as fever because he has no better word for it.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the entry route is food, water, contact, or layered exposure.
sensoryAnchors: borrowed cots in a room never meant to be an infirmary
beats.transitionIntent: investigative carry: the gut-first pattern forces Jaina to compare patient severity against diet and routine while Sylvanas keeps building the timeline outside.

sceneLevelWarnings: Scene 1 rolls straight into Scene 2 without sequel relief; the chapter does not reach its first sequel until beat 7.

scenes.title: Complementary Minds
scenes.sceneFunction: Let each woman solve half the trouble until the halves finally meet.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The contamination is getting into people somehow.
The village's routines may hold the answer.
scenes.arcPressure: The answer only becomes usable once Jaina stops treating diet, routine, and supply as lesser evidence than magic.

enrichment.detail: The plague mechanism here should stay gut-first and exposure-weighted: repeated ingestion produces worse cases, and Jaina is sharp enough to see that the body is participating in the mechanism without solving the full theorem. Antonidas-trained Jaina can make that fast, authoritative read without being allowed the Chapter 12 completion early.
enrichment.source: stories/the-plague-road/plans/plague-mechanism.md; Warcraft Wiki: Jaina (Warcraft III); Wowhead: Lore of Jaina Proudmoore

beats.conflict: Jaina can help some patients temporarily, but she cannot treat the underlying intake while the village keeps eating from the same stores.

outcome.summary: She proves partial intervention is possible, but only as delay, not cure.

beats.valueShift: something is getting into them somehow -> I can buy time, not victory
newOnPageInformation: Stabilization wards can slow progression in some cases.
Most patients are still early enough to help if the entry route is found quickly.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What the patients are all taking in that created the pattern.
beats.transitionIntent: causal carry: temporary stabilization is useless without the intake route, so Jaina cross-checks food habits and household routine next.

beats.conflict: Jaina has to sort the real clue from a pile of ordinary food and habit.

outcome.summary: She proves the contamination enters through ingestion and that heavy bread-eaters are the worst cases.

beats.valueShift: I can buy time, not victory -> the grain is the weapon
newOnPageInformation: Gut concentration is the real clue.
Bread consumption correlates with severity.
Home-grown oats or separate provisions correlate with lighter cases.
Repeated meals matter; this is cumulative settling in the body, not one bad swallow.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Where the tainted grain first joins the local stores.
sensoryAnchors: grain dust on wood and clothes
Jaina gripping the frame when the pattern locks
beats.transitionIntent: revelatory convergence: once grain reads as the carrier, supplier history becomes the next necessary question.

beats.goal: Determine what changed in the village before the outbreak.
beats.conflict: The witnesses only remember routines, prices, deliveries, and practical changes — not magical theory.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas finds the same supplier-and-timeline pattern as Millhaven, but still cannot prove why it matters.

beats.valueShift: the grain is the weapon -> same supplier, same timeline
newOnPageInformation: New grain supplier three months back.
The same 'pleasant, forgettable, better prices' description appears again.
Sylvanas has the people-and-road pattern, but not yet why it matters.
Jaina can see from the window that Sylvanas is building a usable model of the village out of prices, deliveries, and fear.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether this supplier is cause or coincidence.
beats.transitionIntent: revelatory convergence: Sylvanas's supplier pattern has to be laid beside Jaina's ingestion read before either answer is usable alone.
beats.verifierNotes: Strengthen the supplier echo without treating the old seller or the newer grain man as solved identity proof yet.

beats.dilemma: Both women now have partial explanations that fail alone.
beats.decision: Share immediately and act on the combined picture.
beats.valueShift: same supplier, same timeline -> tainted grain is being moved on purpose
newOnPageInformation: Jaina's answer and Sylvanas's answer fit exactly.
The granary is now a crime-scene target, not just a contaminated structure.
They are reading the deployment earlier than the villages themselves, but still after each delivery has already landed.
Jaina catches herself waiting for Sylvanas's half before trusting her own answer, which is new enough to be disorienting.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether they are dealing with a lone saboteur or something larger.
sensoryAnchors: silence before both realize they reached the same picture
beats.transitionIntent: action continuation: with the two halves locked together, they move on the granary before anyone inside can scrub the trail.

scenes.title: The Granary
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn the sickness case into a hunt.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The road and storehouses are spreading it.
scenes.arcPressure: Once the grain answer lands, Jaina has to accept that logistics, evidence handling, and field timing are part of the mechanism rather than afterthoughts.

enrichment.detail: Andorhal is Lordaeron's breadbasket and distribution hub: warehouse-scale granaries, loading yards, grain dust, and outbound wagon traffic feeding whole regions. This village granary should feel like one spoke in that larger supply web, which sharpens why the next move is southwest toward the hub rather than staying local forever.

beats.goal: Secure the grain stores and see how the grain is being doctored.
beats.conflict: A young watcher inside the granary reaches for a lamp to destroy the evidence.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas stops the fire and Jaina contains the broken agent, but the presence of a live watcher proves deliberate conspiracy.

beats.valueShift: someone is moving tainted grain on purpose -> someone is guarding the operation
newOnPageInformation: The contamination exists in concentrated liquid form before mixing.
A live operative is stationed at village level to monitor the weapon.
sensoryAnchors: Sylvanas's arrow pinning instead of killing
beats.transitionIntent: action continuation: a live captured watcher turns evidence control into prisoner control and urgent analysis.

beats.goal: Secure the prisoner and understand what the vessel means.
beats.conflict: The initiate is terrified, not disciplined-looking, and that makes him easy to underestimate while Jaina is forced to triage evidence fast.

outcome.summary: They physically hold him, and Jaina proves the liquid is skilled work rather than hedge poison; sack marks and ledger notes point southwest through Andorhal, but they still do not get usable words out of him.

beats.valueShift: someone is guarding the operation -> this is bigger than one frightened boy
newOnPageInformation: The alchemical design is far beyond hedge work.
The treatment sits on necromantic work Jaina knows only from warnings and half-whispered lessons about things Dalaran does not teach openly.
Recent sack marks and granary ledger notes tie the latest shipment lane southwest through Andorhal.
The initiate is a cog, not a mastermind.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who above the village line is directing the route.
Whether Andorhal is source, hub, or only the next visible node.
sensoryAnchors: Cyndia and Lyriel moving the prisoner under armed control
beats.transitionIntent: causal snap: the prisoner reaches his poison before a real interrogation can begin, collapsing the easy route to names.
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina already knows necromancy in the broad Kirin Tor sense; the novelty here is the skilled applied design and delivery method, not the subject itself.
beats.verifierModification: Added a concrete Andorhal-bearing logistics clue so the chapter5 handoff comes from observed evidence instead of authorial push.

beats.goal: Interrogate the initiate before the trail goes cold.
beats.conflict: The initiate kept a concealed poison capsule for capture, making the window between restraint and questioning shorter than ordinary custody can solve.

outcome.summary: The witness is dead before they can break him, and the hidden capsule makes the silence feel trained rather than accidental.

beats.valueShift: this is bigger than one frightened boy -> the village is tied to something larger and colder
newOnPageInformation: He carried a concealed poison capsule specifically for capture.
The village line is disciplined enough to train disposable watchers to die before talking.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who is directing the route beyond Andorhal.
sensoryAnchors: warm body on the cell floor
beats.transitionIntent: decision carryover: with the watcher dead, only recorded logistics, grain marks, and the southwest supply chain remain actionable.
beats.verifierModification: Made the poison mechanism explicit so the death reads as enemy discipline instead of escort incompetence.

beats.dilemma: Stay and keep helping a village that still thinks it has a fever problem, or leave partial relief behind and go after the source before more places become Millhaven.
beats.decision: Seal what grain they can, leave hard containment instructions with the captain and medic, and follow the grain network toward Andorhal.
beats.valueShift: the village is tied to something larger and colder -> the trail has a destination
newOnPageInformation: The source is no longer just an idea; records and sack marks tie the route southwest through Andorhal.
Sylvanas leaves the captain with orders to seal the granary and pull suspect grain out of circulation.
Jaina leaves the medic strict instructions about bread exposure, triage order, and which patients may still be slowed.
Jaina and Sylvanas are now solving the same problem from opposite ends, and the picture only clears when they set their answers together.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Andorhal is the source or only the next visible tier.
sensoryAnchors: imagined grain wagons already moving southwest
beats.transitionIntent: forward hook: chapter5 opens with the quartet following records, storehouses, and wagon routes toward Andorhal.
beats.verifierNotes: Keep the closing shift functional rather than warm; this is method alignment, not trust.
beats.verifierModification: Weighted the departure choice with concrete local containment measures so the pursuit does not erase the village stakes.

factsLockedForChapter5: Grain is confirmed as what carries the plague into people.
The road and storehouse network is how it spreads.
A live village watcher existed and died by poison before he could be questioned.
Granary records and sack marks tie the next target to the route southwest through Andorhal.
Millhaven remains the first fully claimed village in the pattern; most other settlements are still only in confused early-stage sickness.
Millhaven left two weak grain-side anomalies in the file: the old seller on the north road and the survivor girl's memory of a newer grain man.
factsNotYetEarned: The Barov family connection as certainty.
Recognition of the old grain seller.
Any collapse of the partnership into open trust.

normalizations: Normalized beatType and scenePhase from the markdown beat headings.
Converted meta-info references, knowledge ledger, arc position, and cast/handoff notes into preserved JSON scaffold form.
Added scene-level arcPressure where the chapter's stance test is visibly active.
Kept the active outline title as operative authority; older arc docs still carry the stale title 'The Dying Village'.
warnings: Beats 1-6 run six consecutive scene beats before the first sequel at beat 7. The accumulating procedural pressure works, but the chapter stays scene-heavy for a long stretch.
Scene outcomes skew heavily toward yes-but (beats 1-4, 6, and 9), with only one clean yes and two no-and turns. The chapter still lands, but the tension texture is narrower than it could be.

Cross-doc drift: story-arc.md and investigation-arc.md still call Chapter 4 'The Dying Village' while the active outline is 'The Sick Village'. Kept the outline title as operative authority for this run.

chapterBridge: Intentional two-day bridge from chapter3's southbound recommitment: the same reduced quartet arrives carrying Millhaven's proof, Neral's absence, and the weak grain clue into a village still alive enough to misname the plague as ordinary sickness.

craftResearch.findings: The chapter needs a fair late pivot built from planted evidence, not a conclusion dropped in from outside the scene.
craftResearch.source: Jane Friedman, Novel Factory, Wattpad Creators thriller and mystery craft guidance via web research

craftResearch.findings: Long procedural or action runs need reaction and decision space or the pressure turns flat instead of sharp.
craftResearch.source: Dabble, Writers Helping Writers, The Novelry scene-sequel guidance via web research

craftResearch.findings: Transitions land best when they carry explicit causal or emotional momentum and orient the reader cleanly across time or place changes.
craftResearch.source: Authors Pathway, Blue Pencil, Helping Writers Become Authors transition guidance via web research

craftResearch.findings: Mystery discovery works best when magical logic stays rule-bound, clues escalate complication, and personal stakes stay attached to each reveal.
craftResearch.source: Write It Scared, Master of Worlds, Sudowrite thriller craft guidance via web research

pacing.notes: The chapter runs hot through beat 7, then uses beat 11 as the necessary release-and-redirection beat. The pressure is deliberate rather than shapeless, but it only stays sharp once the exit choice carries real local cost.
details: Beat 1 opens inside motion, coughs, and buried wrongness instead of setup summary.
Beats 1 through 3 escalate by access problem, then body read, not by repeating the same note.
Beats 4 through 6 stay scene-heavy, but each changes the investigative method from treatment, to diagnosis, to logistics.
Beat 7 is the chapter's first real breath, and it arrives just before the granary run can turn monotonous.
Beats 8 through 10 create the chapter's tightest pressure cluster, so beat 11 has to absorb consequence and choice rather than merely point south.

structure.notes: The chapter escalates from living-village unease, to grain confirmation, to deliberate conspiracy, then hands off cleanly into the next pursuit. The major structural fix was earning Andorhal on-page and weighting the departure decision.
details: The opening hook is strong because the village is functioning badly, not already dead.
The middle turn lands at beats 5 through 7, where grain stops being a weak clue and becomes usable proof.
Scene 2 is the hinge because Jaina and Sylvanas solve separate halves before the chapter allows convergence.
The granary sequence works as the climax because it converts diagnosis into opposition, not just more evidence.
The close survives because the witness death narrows the route to logistics and pursuit instead of wrapping the case.

character.notes: Jaina stays active, limited-third holds, and the arc block is delivered without softening the Jaina-Sylvanas lane into trust. The chapter's gains come from method correction, not emotional thaw.
details: Beat 1 filters the village through Jaina's wrongness-read rather than detached worldbuilding.
Beat 2 corrects Jaina's view of Sylvanas through observed behavior, which keeps POV discipline intact.
Beats 3 through 5 give Jaina agency in diagnosis, triage, and proof instead of leaving her reactive.
Beat 6 keeps Sylvanas load-bearing while still filtering the discovery through what Jaina can see and infer.
Beat 7 is the chapter's forced-choice beat: Jaina stops protecting a neat magical lane and acts on the combined method.
Beat 11 lands the promised end-state only if the prose keeps the shift cooler than trust and sharper than camaraderie.

transitions.notes: All 11 beat handoffs were checked and retyped into explicit causal carries. The chapter bridge now marks the intentional two-day gap, and the granary run no longer leaps to Andorhal without a visible link.
details: Beat 1 now hands by dialogue pressure into the captain confrontation instead of a generic next-step note.
Beat 3 now names the investigative carry from gut pattern to diet review, which keeps scene 2 from feeling like a restart.
Beat 5 now points causally toward supplier history, making beat 6 feel earned rather than adjacent.
Beat 6 now hands by convergence into beat 7, clarifying why the chapter needs a shared-read sequel before the granary.
Beat 9 now snaps causally into beat 10 through the concealed poison, which closes the interrogation window without making the escort look careless.
Beat 11 now hands chapter5 a forward hook anchored in records, route, and pursuit rather than in abstract intention.

continuity.notes: The chapter advances prior state cleanly: the quartet stays consistent, grain moves from weak clue to earned answer, and the Jaina-Sylvanas shift remains operational rather than warm. No contradiction with memory indexes was found.
details: Beat 1 evolves Millhaven's aftermath into the first living village in the pattern without replaying chapter3.
Beats 2, 7, and 11 advance the Jaina-Sylvanas lane from proof-tested alignment into colder method dependence, not trust.
Beats 3 through 5 evolve the living-contamination-vector thread from suggestive grain clue to explicit ingestion proof.
Beat 6 strengthens the newer-grain-seller thread but keeps the earlier road anomalies as weak scraps, not solved identity proof.
Beat 9 guards against anti-reintroduction by treating broad necromancy knowledge as established and the applied design as the new layer.
The chapter never reintroduces Neral or the survivor girl on-stage, preserving chapter3's locked departures.
Callback density remains at 0 percent because the chapter spends its beats advancing prior material instead of recapping it.

modifications.description: Retyped all 11 transitionIntent fields into explicit causal carries so every beat hands the next beat or scene a clear reason to exist.
Added Andorhal-bearing sack marks and ledger notes to beat 9 so the chapter5 pursuit is grounded in discovered evidence.
Clarified the concealed poison capsule in beat 10 and the necromantic-knowledge boundary in beat 9 so the chapter preserves escort competence and Jaina's established knowledge level.
Expanded beat 11 so the departure toward Andorhal leaves concrete containment orders behind, preserving the village stakes while still launching the next pursuit.
